item.title: Community pulse: what r/3Dprinting is debating this week
item.description: The arguments, consensus shifts, and quietly useful threads from the upgrades community this week.

item.title: The design checklist: run this before you spend
item.description: Seven questions that separate a justified design purchase from an expensive mood.

item.title: Five mistakes everyone makes in their first month of filament
item.description: The most common early stumbles in filament, and the cheap corrections that fix them for home 3D-printing hobbyists.

item.title: "3d print stringing fix" in practice: a real-world field test
item.description: We put the most-repeated advice about 3d print stringing fix through a month of actual use. Some of it survived.

item.title: Ask the forge: your upgrades questions, answered
item.description: Straight answers to the upgrades questions home 3D-printing hobbyists keep asking, minus the hedging.

item.title: The quiet upgrade: small tweaks in design with outsized returns
item.description: No purchases, no overhauls — the modest design adjustments that home 3D-printing hobbyists report mattering most.

item.title: Myth vs. reality: 3d print stringing fix
item.description: The persistent myths around 3d print stringing fix, stress-tested against what home 3D-printing hobbyists actually report.

item.title: A weekend project: level up your calibration in two hours
item.description: One contained, satisfying calibration project for home 3D-printing hobbyists — start Saturday morning, done by lunch.
